annotations.grafana.app/message: Increase default time-range for the dashboard

spec.expr:
100 *
sum(
  count_over_time(
    {job="nginx"}
    | json
    | __error__ = ""
    | is_probe = 0
    | geoip_country_name = "Russia"
    | request_uri =~ "^/publications(\\?.*)?$"
    | request_uri !~ "(?i).*(\\.env|\\.php|wp-admin|wp-login|xmlrpc).*"
    | request_uri !~ "(wp-|\\.php|\\.env|xmlrpc|admin)"
    | status >= 200 and status < 400
    [$__range]
  )
)
/
sum(
  count_over_time(
    {job="nginx"}
    | json
    | __error__ = ""
    | is_probe = 0
    | geoip_country_name = "Russia"
    | request_uri =~ "^/publications(\\?.*)?$"
    | request_uri !~ "(?i).*(\\.env|\\.php|wp-admin|wp-login|xmlrpc).*"
    | request_uri !~ "(wp-|\\.php|\\.env|xmlrpc|admin)"
    [$__range]
  )
)

options.content:
## TERS-Team Hybrid Synthetic SLA

**Global Synthetic SLA** is sourced from Grafana Synthetic Monitoring for `/` and `/publications`.

**RU/CN Reachability** is sourced from real-user Loki evidence.

Interpret RU/CN panels together with sample size:
- `No data` means there were no qualifying real-user requests in the selected time range.
- low sample count means the percentage is weak evidence
- use **2d** for operational view and **7d** for broader reachability context

spec.expr:
100 *
sum(
  count_over_time(
    {job="nginx"}
    | json
    | __error__ = ""
    | is_probe = 0
    | geoip_country_name = "Russia"
    | request_uri =~ "^/$"
    | request_uri !~ "(?i).*(\\.env|\\.php|wp-admin|wp-login|xmlrpc).*"
    | request_uri !~ "(wp-|\\.php|\\.env|xmlrpc|admin)"
    | status >= 200 and status < 400
    [$__range]
  )
)
/
sum(
  count_over_time(
    {job="nginx"}
    | json
    | __error__ = ""
    | is_probe = 0
    | geoip_country_name = "Russia"
    | request_uri =~ "^/$"
